channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 71 replies — 284.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-03-17 23:53 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

61996036
>>61993232
Can't expect a massive inflow like BTC. Had a decent pump already.

The technicals look stable. Advice. Jump in now while you can

61997405
100$ end of bull run

61997626
Just bought in, life savings. When does it hit $10?

item.title: What's the best trade/career to get into if you're a 30+ year old NEET who suddenly has to work? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the best trade/career to get into if you're a 30+ year old NEET who suddenly has to work?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-17 23:56 UTC

OP

What's the best trade/career to get into if you're a 30+ year old NEET who suddenly has to work?

Asking for a friend.

Early replies (first 3)

61997399
Anything where do you don't have to interact with hr women. They reject chuds because you give them the ick

61997418
>>61997380
why would you start waging? are you ill?

61997474
>>61997380
We don't want incel chuds.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61997485
>>61997380
better cancel that friendship before he get you a job too

61997488
>>61997380
have you ever worked before? do you have a degree?

61997541
>>61997380
Stop asking

61997636
>>61997380
dick sucking under bridges
your initial investment should be a pair of kneepads

item.title: It takes HYPE, a perp dex protocol making billions in revenue with 0 VC overhang and a 30% airdrop worth billions at pea… — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It takes HYPE, a perp dex protocol making billions in revenue with 0 VC overhang and a 30% airdrop worth billions at pea…

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-03-17 23:43 UTC

OP

It takes HYPE, a perp dex protocol making billions in revenue with 0 VC overhang and a 30% airdrop worth billions at peak value doing basically everything right and in good faith by an 11 man pseudo-anon team to just BARELY flip Cardano.

A 10 year old ghostchain with 0 users, 0 revenue, 0 apps with the founder regularly flexing his blackhawk helicopters and wealth. And all it did was launch at the perfect time in 2017 where it managed to build an entire generation of baggies who still believe in the token

Early replies (first 3)

61997496
>>61997484
>And all it did was launch at the perfect time in 2017
it also targeted the japanese market at launch

61997516
>>61997484
Dex protocols are pretty high risk.

61997580
>>61997516
>muh GMX
Hype eats Binance and CZ knows this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 87 replies — 166.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-03-17 23:25 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

61993148
>>61990673
Certified non-argument haver coping by fully avoiding the substance and hoping rhetoric games about how I am cringe or something for actually being substantial will outweigh the truth mattering. Couldn't be me.

61993164
>>61990596
You are literallly retarded. Ripple Treasury (formerly Gtreasury) routes through and is connected to SWIFT. Nowhere did I or anyone claim SWIFT is part of the stack they built. You zoomed in on something you aren't even right about and ignored the graphic portraying exactly what I claimed to cope. You are currently trying to for the 100th thread after acting like Ripple sales of XRP are securities pretend that directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time and does not impact price at all is "selling to retail." It is so disingenuous and faggoted it is kind of literally unbelievable how low you are willing to diminish yourself rather than just accepting reality. You are past the point of even vaguely reasonable critique and have let yourself become a weird person because you were OBSESSED with a fucking crypto project that you were wrong about. SO insane. I cannot even imagine being like this.

61993236
>>61993164
When will xrp moon? Me want money

61993358
>>61993164
>directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time

You're describing how all sales work. You can't sell without buyers "of exactly equivalent size".
You're pic related. You're the meme.

Also, blind sales via exchanges = sales to retail.

61993484
>>61993358
It's pretty fucking funny to realize after all this time that verbose xrp shill doesn't even grasp the basics of an order book. I stopped engaging with him half a year ago and I still feel stupid for having wasted time arguing with this mouth breather. Your patience is legendary anon.

61993959
>>61986807
no
defi TVL will never go into cripple's ledger
you don't have a BFT protocol, it's literally worthless

>>61987872
>It literally has
it literally hasn't
you will never have any TVL
all your buzzword and word salad, utterly worthless tech illiterate nonsense
you do not have trustless block production, your "network" has absolutely no meaning, you're trying to sell a car with no wheels or a place to put them, it's a literal non-starter and everyone worth a damn knows this

enjoy your slow crawl out of the top300

61994433
>>61993358
No, you literal ironic fucking retard, you do not. Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched for their institutional client through the exchange with it to hit the order book at the exact same time, exactly equating the water analogy I mentioned. Insane you are pretending to not understand this.
>>61993959
Something isn't word salad just because you are unable to respond to it and that frustrates you. You are not entitled to automatic rightness and editor of reality fit to render things incoherent which aren't. Ripple already has TVL despite literally being designed to not have L1 smartcontracts etc. Lol attacing us for having byzantine agreement instead of proof of stake/work is not a deep attack and the funniest thing of your reply is you mirroring the terminology I use. You try to start it by having me be dismissed as incoherent and not considered because you can't address my points (conceding you are insecure in your argument/position) and you then supplicate yourself to me mirroring my language use signaling that you understand my competence and wish to try to conform to it and internalize it by imitative association. You can't fake it til you make it here and you demonstrate yourself to be a literal NPC. Enjoy being a literal cult member to a tech that solves no problem, and inserts friction into the broader software solution as a literal jewish rent seeking fee insertion with no purpose beyond this and being sold to you to fund the software company. Just XRP's recent move over the past few days of +11% up is several billion dollars more than link's entire marketcap. Pic related is since the Trump pump.

61995144
>>61994433
>Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched
Yes, that's how selling and buying works.
You're in here pretending that Ripple invented the order book, holy kek.

This is the level of IQ keeping Ripple afloat.

>>61993484
I remember a time when saying something that stupid got you dogpiled on here. Did this guy simply out-retard everyone through attrition, like he did with you?

61995184
>>61972246
the absolute irony of complaining about the middleman while being completely superflous in a world of atomic swaps

or what, do you think institutions will swap their stablecoins with XRP inbetween? lol good thing we have XRP, as we all know, USDT (usd) to EURC (euro) isn't possible with this godlike 8R1DGE CURR3NCY

61996022
>>61995184
Newest amendment enables atomic swaps so yes this would be possible.

61996415
>>61994433
no TVL
no defi
no institutional TVL
no RWA

it's worthless, it doesn't have trustless ordering (BFT), it's a ghostchain that will never be used by anything/anyone
you don't use it today, you never will

61996428
>>61995184
how much in fee do you pay per stable transaction? oh that's right, quite a bit, you little cuck

61996441
the culmination of years of cripple development

61997116
>>61996441
Nigger defilama is not up to date.
Try this https://app.rwa.xyz/networks/xrp-ledger
Y
So bad at fudding its hilarious

61997303
>>61997116
Nigger that's like 1% market share.

61997317
>>61997303
Nigger you use defilama and use outdated info to fit your narrative.
From 0 to 1% , what else are you going to weasle out of.

61997361
>>61997317
>outdated info
That Defillama info isn't outdated.
Neither is that 1% RWA market share.

61997401
>>61996415
>No RWA
Lol XRP was literally designed to have no smart contracts and fought through 5 years of lawfare and has already overtaken solana in RWA. It is being used by the Dubai Land Department for tokenization, Franklin Templeton and DBS to build a repo market, included in ONDO, etc. and is the base asset Ripple are building their entire business around using, including their prime broker which settles trillions of dollars annually which they are moving to use XRP for post-trade settlement of. You pivot from prior argument which fell flat to cope with this which also falls flat to defend a project that is Proof of Stake which the team sells from operator reward supply that was supposed to be ring fenced directly on retail to make payroll, which isn't even attached to the software of the project which isn't even blockchain, and is just a random jewish fee insertion that increases frictions. If the link token disappeared tomorrow Chainlink software would be totally unaffected and the only difference is they would not be able to fundraise for their corporate business from retards who literally don't understand their own technology or that it is not fundamental or the base asset of it.

61997411
>>61997401
>market share: 0.52%

61997420
>>61995184
It isn't superfluous retard. We have had this discussion extensively in several dozen threads. Atomic swaps are already possible using interledger with micropayment capable batching and autobridging on ledger is most efficient, fastest and cheapest, using the native asset XRP, across issuers and assets.
>>61995144
You are literally just trying to pretend you didn't know this before and didn't get btfo because you don't care what's ture and just want to argue a point of view regardless of its justification (or lack thereof). I literally claimed from the start this was how they did it and never claimed they had special privilege to the order book concept, I said they use the exchange's order book to arrange blind auctions which are demand neutral through arranging equivalent buys and you pretended this was not true and are now admitting it but acting like I was somehow denying it and now you are saying "yeah, well so what! Checkmate cripplet!" Lol Become a real person again.

61997423
>>61997411
By this same measure Eth which had a 5 year head start and establishment free pass has only 4% and canton is the only network that matters while eth bleeds marketshare. Lol Sure that's the argument you wanna make?

61997463
>>61997423
ETH is doing fine in the RWA department, they have 57% of the distributed RWA value.

61997490
>>61997463
>Distributed
Why would this matter more than all? Lol The DTCC is using canton. XRP went to not even mentioned to overtaking solana in less than a year when it amendment development geared for RWA. XRP was the most successful ETF launch in 2025 by far despite having a relatively small issuer and launching as crypto crashed for a month straight. Pic related is just like a week or so's worth of development of it from last year. Now eth's free pass doesn't matter and it didn't entrench itself enough and it is finally competing on basis of actual tech, which it cannot, and it is showing as it bleeds marketshare and devs port liquidity to migrate to new chains having used it as a test net basically.

61997500
>>61997463
The update on those EFTs mentioned in >>61997490. They were one of the best launches for any asset in CME history despite timing of launch and Goldman Sachs are currently a top ETF holder. ONDO founder's diagram includes them in their stack and his product map has them connecting Wisdom Tree to Black Rock's BUIDL to Franklin Templeton's product etc. While eth and sol etc. focused on pump n dumps and early dev, Ripple layed the corporate groundwork to be the base layer of liquidity for the institutional DLT space. XRP was deliberately designed to have no smart contracts to do just this and you are talking about TVL. Lol

item.title: JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm… — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-03-17 23:48 UTC

OP

JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC
issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital commodity.

Early replies (first 3)

61997106
Source: https://x.com/chainlink/status/2034020963952869473

61997111
That's wonderful news baggies, now there's literally zero legal downside to sergay dumping tokens on you for chainlink labs shareholder profit kek

61997112
*crashes to heckin zero in your path*

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61997129
no one cares. next time post the price and we can go from there.

61997131
this is for most crypto not just link. lol

61997132
>>61997123
>Innocent people will get missiles dropped on their head because of this
Absolutely. I expect the war to escalate bigly thanks to this. Watch out!

61997148
>Slowly, then all at once

61997162
>>61997131
Yes it's good news for mostly everyone.
APT
AVAX
BTC
BCH
ADA
LINK
DOGE
ETH
HBAR
LTC
DOT
SHIB
SOL
XLM
XTZ
XRP
So we can finally move on from the security FUD.
The clarity act is next.

61997166
>>61997102
HOLY FUCK THIS IS IT BOYS THIS IS THE BIG ONE OH SHIT OH FUCK OH IM GONNA COOOOOM

61997171
>>61997162
Yes no more securities FUD, now just a completely useless market with zero investor protections, I'm sure that will really save crypto

61997176
>>61997171
Save crypto from what? Crypto is fine.

61997199
>>61997162
HOW IS SHIBA AND DOGE STILL GETTING MENTIONED IN SHIT LIKE THIS IN THE YEAR 2026 THIS IS SOOOOOOO GAY

61997202
>>61997176
Oh shit, it's retarded. Might want to review the trash volume and collapsing use cases

61997212
>>61997199
High mcaps make them relevant in a way.
>>61997202
bot

61997227
>>61997102
...it's literally going back to $5 isn't it?

61997242
>>61997162
omg the trash on this list... it's over

61997267
>>61997212
>I DONT LIKE IT BOT BOT BOT
Cryptos over, imagine thinking instutions will invest into an industry where insiders can legally rugpull them since it's not a security lmao

61997271
>>61997267
>rugpull
Just fuck off you retarded bag of shit

61997276
>>61997271
Not an argument, good try though brainlet

61997282
>>61997199
they have lobbyists

61997300
>>61997227
>$5
That's being generous, how does $00.00 sound?

61997318
>>61997267
We went from
>chainlink bad because its a security
To
>chainlink bad because its not a security
I honestly hope you trannies get stabbed my niggers

61997324
>>61997162
What the helly are those tickers unc....This nigga might be a time traveler from 2021?

61997347
And yet we're down 2% today. Lol. LMAO even

61997356
>>61997347
Just retards selling the news. They’ll buy back higher

61997364
>>61997102
As usual CLL try to hype something to look like it pertains to specifically them. They issued a statement on crypto in general not even mentioning link, which itself has nothing to do with the Chainlink software which is not even blockchain and is a completely detached token that is injected as a totally unnecessary friction

61997376
>>61997318
I haven't changed my viewpoint and warned everyone since the Gensler shitposts. God help you if you can't figure it out on your own.

61997415
>>61997364
This gay fud doesn’t work anymore now the payment abstraction is online

61997436
>>61997102
Awwww sheeeeiiiit

61997446
>>61997364
See
>>61997162
And dilate

61997473
THAT NIGGER ARTEMIS WAS RIGHT! NOW COMPANIES CAN BUY THIS SHIT AT LARGE

61997514
>>61997162
Everyone else on that list is so Goyim don’t buy Link.

61997604
>>61997162
weird how it's not Ripple (XRP) and just XRP

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 65 replies — 35.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-03-17 22:07 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

Early replies (first 3)

61990544
Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp

61990577
>>61990540
It’s gonna dump soon. We have been doing this dance for almost a decade now. Sadly there is not much to expect until we have actual real life adoption.

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993457
>>61993441
LIES! BEGONE, HERETIC!

61993499
>>61993457
AHHHH A GAY GUY

61993511
>Got 10k linkies in my early twenties
>Boughted since then and am now early 30s and have 100k link
It's been so fucking long, really hope we will be awarded in the end.

61993517
>>61993046
the only one that ever mattered was 42. and he is no where to be found since 2021...

61993549
>>61993511
Unironically I hope so, anon

61993565
>>61993511
anon you'll make it when link tops at $70 well played

61993585
>>61993307
Very reddit reply

61993655
>>61993046
I want to believe
Never selling

61993950
>>61993046
You made me lose a lot of money. Shame on you.

61994019
>>61993046
Artemis you have been the lamest larper

61994053
>>61993046
kys

61994074
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BLs_NP0A1Vw

we are gonna make it

61994082
I boughteded more frens
Missed february cheapies because my truck turned gay but we're back

61994241
>>61993517
>aroha

61994249
>>61990540
XRP is mogging us, it’s up 10% we are only up 4%. By the way 10% for XRP is ~10 billion in increased mcap more than chainlinks market cap….

61994261
>>61990544
Can someone share or link his prediction I need some copium rn

61994286
>>61994261

61994326
>>61994286

61994330
>>61994326
Where’s the March prediction

61994642
YOU GOTTA FIGHT
FOR YOUR RIGHT
TO HATE LIIIIINKIIIIEES

61994646
>>61993046

an hero NOW

61994741
>>61990540
I don't even care if I make it I just want to see my GME baggie friends cope and seethe by glorious LINK price action

61994752
>>61993046
Since you're here, please tell me... are they planning to implement a "whitelist" for stakers? Like, only approved entities can stake? It's been my deepest fear about the delays to staking.

61994758
>>61994752
of course. what... you thought.. LMAO.. you were gonna get to stake? with the big boys? you stupid neet ass faggot LMAO ROFL LMAO

61995921
>>61993046
I predict: -2.4%

61995937
>>61990540
This year for sure

61996432
just got to 14k linkies, keep stackin

61997084
I AM SORRY FOR DOUBTING I KNEEL
https://x.com/chainlink/status/2034020963952869473

61997164
>>61993046
was the sec news what you were referring to or something bigger?? how important was it for clarity that link is a digital commodity?

item.title: YLYL — 96 replies — 23.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
YLYL

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-03-17 19:52 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61970837
>>61970830
FUCK LOST ON FPBP

61970841
>>61970825

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61994944
>>61994920
>reddit gives nigger bad advice
>he gets rekt over it
>reddit hides his replies with downvotes
Lololol

61995758
>>61994238
>>61994913
did lol

>>61994777
kekked and checked

>>61994379
just sad.

>>61994906
based if true

61995812
>>61995774
>one turkroach gooner fudster single handedly destroyed a WEF cult
I love this timeline

61995892
>>61995812

61995935
Pool Status?

61995950
>2026
>still chained to these tokens
The ride never ends

61996186
>>61995965
>trying this hard

61996551
>>61970837
i lost hard, even though i knew what was gonna happen, that was great.

61996569
>>61973123
please be real, got a link?

61996575
>>61994101
its a holdover thing from poor working class boomers, my grandparents had a similar photo with their second last car they ever bought. They weren't retards like this though, they bought a sensible car for a sensible price.

61996581
>>61996569
https://3dvf.com/en/they-spent-e1300-on-electricity-in-an-airbnb-the-owner-discovers-they-were-mining-cryptocurrencies/

61996602
>>61995758
>$250,000 loan
I’ll have more saved up by that age, that’s a drop in a bucket for a billionaire lol

61996607
>>61994075
they're niggers, they're gonna make 1-2 payments then close the account it withdraws from, it doesnt matter how much they agree to pay, the banks deal with the bullshit loans, the car yards already made their money.

This shit should be banned, because it results in white taxpayers having to foot the bill eventually.

>>61996581
no anon, i meant the image, of course some sperg would take their mining rig with them while on holiday and just plug it in.

61996696
>>61994906
i've heard about this lmao. This is the sort of crime i approve of, if your company is dumb enough to pay this, fuck em.

61996722
no clue why people hate on J$
he's the literal embodiment of the judeo-american dream of being a rich pimp

item.title: What do I do? — 27 replies — 23.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do I do?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-03-17 22:47 UTC

OP

>35 soon
>120k NW
Do I just keep paupermaxxing and dumping all my savings into my brokerage account + maxxing my Roth every year?

I save about 2k a month. I’ve become a bit of a stock picking fag, but I want to know if I should just dump the 2k in VT instead of trying to beat the market. My IQ is about 107 and I’m a lazy and unambitious retard so my only “skill” is pauperism

Early replies (first 3)

61995651
>>61995637
>Roth
>thinking you’ll have the privilege to live to retirement age
Bigger scam than life insurance

61995663
>>61995637
Wait until you have 200k and retire in Latin America/SEA/non-EU Eastern Europe.
>nooo I don't want to live in first world
You said "unambitious" right there. Pick the easy life or continue to struggle the rest of your life, up to you

61995677
>>61995663
That's my plan as well, I thought I would need 300 or 400k though, do you reckon 200k is enough?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61995685
>>61995637
120k is literally nothing

61995716
>>61995637
Market is overinflated. Just buy 5,000 Link for 40k. And your rich by 2028 at most.

61995720
>>61995677
1. Subtract your current age from average life expectancy age
2. Workout an inflation adjusted annual budget based on the lifestyle you want in the country you want to live in
3. Multiply the cost from 2 by the years in 1
4. You now have how much money you’ll need to live there indefinitely, or at least some sort of approximation

61995734
>>61995677
200k is enough for soft retirement, let it stack up whilst you work part time remote or some shit

61995748
>>61995637
Test.

61995750
>>61995637
>just dump the 2k in VT instead of trying to beat the market
Retirement accounts should be in index funds or retirement date funds, especially for midwits.
If you're all ready maxing out your 401k and IRA and have anything left over, you can gamble with that.

61995764
>>61995750
I’m only maxxing out my Roth. ~2k goes in my brokerage

61995783
>>61995750
Usually you want to max out tax advantaged accounts first.
Are you at least getting the full employer match from your 401k?
Are you saving for any big purchases like a house?

61995788
>>61995764
>>61995783
Meant for you

61995900
>>61995734
how is 200k enough to retire in SA wtf that is nothing are u gonna live in the ghetto in some third world shitthole lmao

61995924
>>61995677
200k should be enough to be a poor NEET without a family. If you want to be well off with a family, I'd say even in second world that starts at 500k

61995945
>>61995900
>are u gonna live in the ghetto in some third world shitthole lmao
Better to retire in a cheap shithole than work in an expensive shithole

61996012
>>61995716
Do stinkies really

61996164
>>61995663
Basically my plan, desu. Moving to D.R. or Mexico City in my 40s and done with this shit.

61996187
>>61995716
Silence, jam jeet.

61996197
>>61995900
200k with a two bucket strategy (60% voo, 40% bonds) is 650$ a month, which is above minimum wage in a lot of countries.

61996265
Test.

61996653
>>61996164
>Mexico City
you'll be dead before 50.

61996719
>>61996653
Yeah, no way I'm retiring in a big city in Latin America. Asia is a lot safer

61996756
>>61996719
Yeah, I'd go Asia. Hopefully I develop jungle fever at some point. Currently I only like white girls.

61996840
>>61995637
Do you own a house? First, you should buy yourself a place to live.

61997187
holy shit, it's the security loser posting on /biz/
>>>/trv/2868174

61997209
>>61997187
btw this dude went and got a hair transplant because he was insecure about not getting enough women's attention with thinning hair.
What women does he interact with, you ask? Literally ONLY Thai whores kek.

61997336
>>61995637
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

item.title: PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN — 24 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-03-17 21:56 UTC

OP

here you go lads, in this canister you can play PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN. A nice side effect of playing PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN is that you get rewarded in ARC token, which can not be bought, only obtained as a reward for playing PACMAN on this canister https://bkysp-nqaaa-aaaam-qh3ia-cai.icp0.io/.
every round of PACMAN costs 0.25 icp, but if anyone rolls trips, i'll lower cost to 0.025 for 24 hours.
there is a leaderboard you can submit your high scores for bragging rights.
the game itself is a buggy mess due to being my first game, also being on a cutting edge technology that is poorly explored, so if you lose money, go fuck yourself!

Early replies (first 3)

61993865
>>61993849
based op

61993869
>>61993849
SAAR!

61993870
>>61993865
ty fren

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993955
1) Pacman is not being played "on the chain" in the sense that the state is not being uploaded on-chain and validated every frame. The game is loaded from the chain and played in the browser.
2) In the unlikely event that your token ever accrued any value, a pacman-playing bot would take over the leaderboard and destroy that value.

61994004
>>61993955
well duh, making 1000's of updates per second would be too expensive. obviously you put parts of it offchain you brainlet. you could very well do it with rust or even motoko for that matter, it's just not economical.
also the payouts are regressive and based on the total amount of tokens in the reward treasury, meaning you can obtain less and less the later you join.

tl;dr SO FUCKING WHAT?!?! its just PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN

61994033
>>61994004
>well duh, making 1000's of updates per second would be too expensive
if there is no on-chain validation, validation is centralized. not only is there is no way to enforce that your game of pacman is being played how you intend, but players themselves cannot verify that they are not being cheated, either by you or by other players exploiting the game. there is no reason to involve a blockchain in this process, since you gain no benefit from using one compared to a centralized server that can at least attempt to filter bots and guarantee that player inputs follow the rules.

61994077
>>61993849
It's nice to see people actually doing stuff on ICP.
Is there somewhere we can see the Pacman game without needing ICP?

61994106
lemme guess... you want me to open that link that looks like it will try to hack me, and then you want me to send you my hard earned money? in exchange i can play pacman that you vibecoded in 10 min? is that the idea?

61994156
>>61994077
the canister is shitting the bed in performance, but here is it anyways

>>61994106
yeah but this way we get to share our scores and play a subpar version of the game TOGETHER

>>61994033
i literally don't care, i had hella fun vibe coding this garbage

61994162
>>61994156
>I don't care how these systems work or what makes them secure or useful
sounds about right yes

61994169
>>61994162
yes

61996018
>>61994004
lol based

61996033
GOT YOUR ASS SHANEONCHAIN

61996133
>>61994169
Actually, anon has a point. if this was a super serious project, you'd want to find a way to prevent users from exploiting the client side game. Not really an issue for a fun play project ofc, but something to think about for the future

61996195
>>61994077
>wowroms

61996214
>>61993849
This kind of initiative is to be commended. Hats off to the artist.

61996608
>>61993849
Hi jerry.

61996711
>>61996133
OP here sucking on a big ol dong.
obviously there is an off chain backend that verifies your movement and score. it also is responsible for the movement of ghosts. this makes the game really buggy. since the backend is off chain you have syncing issues with the canister depending on canister health and general IC network connectivity.
it iz what it iz, but i will not pay for the cycles for 100's of clients making updates 100's/second. maybe someday, if we get significant gaming volume for it to make sense.

currently working on introducing minesweeper to the arcade because it does not need real time connectivity. this could be done with a onchain motoko backend i think.

>>61996214
ty buddy
>>61996608
try again buddy.

61996785
>>61996711
>but i will not pay for the cycles for 100's of clients making updates 100's/second
I'm not asking you to do that, I'm pointing out the absurdity. You have a backend that you control that can forge or disqualify sessions, so why should users pay to play pacman to receive tokens that you disburse? None of it makes sense. Moving on, even though Minesweeper is not real-time, it still requires a secret state unknown to the player, which must be generated and stored somewhere to check moves. It is again extremely difficult to do this without a centralized server that you control, so why involve a blockchain at all?

61996868
>>61996785
newsflash buddy, people right now spend thousands on rigged csgo skin loot boxes or pokemon cards which are all centralized. turns out people do not care about decentralization as they care about having tokens. what you decentralism fags don't understand is that a trustless networks will just incentivize scams. turns out as well that people inherently want to trust other people.
i pinky promise i won't rug the free token without lp. whats the difference in throwing money into an lp? at least you get to play a round of pacman this way.

i am involving the blockchain to progress my skills and understanding of the things i put my money in, so fuck me right?
also it makes the whole token thing possible. also icp allows me to host stuff on a cloud without a (((credit card))) which is a banger service by itself.

61996931
>>61996868
>newsflash buddy, people right now spend thousands on rigged csgo skin loot boxes or pokemon cards which are all centralized. turns out people do not care about decentralization as they care about having tokens.
we're not in disagreement here, centralized servers are far more efficient for this purpose and players have already demonstrated that they are far more willing to trust a developer than a smart contract. blockchain simply introduces more friction for no benefit.
>I am involving the blockchain to progress my skills
that's great, I've done the same thing. the only issue is that you've elected to charge real money for this product, indicating you believe it is safe for production use, which is not true, and few people in the real world are going to give you that feedback, so I'm going to do it, since you are learning.
>also icp allows me to host stuff on a cloud
that's neat.

61996965
>>61993849
Damn, no shit? I can really play Pacman in brouza in 2026????? FUUUUUKKKK

61997061
>>61993849
I thought it was the arch package manager. Imagine my disappointment

61997136
>>61996931
>icp
>real money
kek, i get it, you may be partially right, but you're not considering the comparative aspect of products on a market. there are a ton of tokens on the market, but only one right now that is only obtainable through playing this subpar version of pacman. the alternative is to buy shitcoin#2billion without a cool dev that makes cool products. so the canister does not need to be safe, its just a fun way to disburse them.
i tell you what, you post your account principal from the pacman canister and, and i send you .75 icp covering for three rounds. whaddya say?

>>61997061
you mean a pacman mirror on icp? interesting

item.title: How the FUCK do I buy BTC without going through an exchange? — 12 replies — 8.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
How the FUCK do I buy BTC without going through an exchange?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-17 22:35 UTC

OP

How the FUCK do I buy BTC without going through an exchange? I don't want to hand over my ID to kikes.

>muh crypto ATM

enjoy your 50% fee. I really don't want to miss out on this generational level bullrun with BTC and I have like $7,400 to buy with. I also live in leafland btw if that helps.

Early replies (first 3)

61996322
they already have every piece of information about you. there is literally nothing to hide.

61996351
time travel

61996519
would probably be very difficult and it also makes it harder to sell later on.
Maybe you already have a brokerage account or your bank lets you buy stocks? ibit would be an option then.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996546
>>61996316
SORRY, KID...!
No ID, no BTC

61996614
Just buy some? Like from someone IRL. It's not that complicated.
>$7.4k canadian funny money
That's nothing dude just check craigslist

61996615
>>61996519
>ibit would be an option

kys

61996645
just offer someone you know a whopper menu for selling you some

61996654
>>61996316
I think the only ways would be to
A. Mine bitcoin
B. Offer illegal services and accept payment in monero, to then swap them for bitcoin on a defi exchange.

61996693
>>61996654
> accept payment in Bitcoin

ftfy

Never go full retard.

61996993
Kycnot.me

61997014
>>61996316
only true way to be anon would be to buy from a random person cash to cash.
every other way gives a piece of your info, be it number, id, social secuirty.
If you live in a a big city you would have no problems finding a person to buy crypto from.in localcoinswap.

61997285
>>61996316
>vote better so free people get a fair shake
>realize voting won't change anything
>re-post this question in a few year or do the needful option
choices choices

item.title: REMEMBER THIS THREAD — 37 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
REMEMBER THIS THREAD

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-03-17 19:41 UTC

OP

WASTE 95% OF YOUR WEALTH ON AN6THING NOW. IF YOU ARE RICH, THERE IS A REVOLUTION AGAINST THE RICH COMING...

61987006
>>61987000

61987019
>>61987000
Che...chee.. checked.

61987033
>>61987000
I wish. People are too brainwashed pr comfortable to give a shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61988103
>>61988091
how much do you make a year?

61988108
>>61987082
You are on the wrong side, goober.
Reconsider your views.

61988111
>>61988103
I make 696,969 a year selling dragon dildos to weak faggot communist fembois online

61988116
>>61988091
Start prepping and head for the woods

61988124
>>61988103
He isn’t poor. Thats for sure.

61988126
>>61988111
why don't poorfags ever answer this question?

61988132
>>61988124
it's hilarious that he thinks $700k/yr is rich. like that's his tongue-in-cheek response. >>61988111 i'm guessing he makes between $60k and $80k a year IRL.

61988144
>>61988126
For all you know my answer could be 99% true. For all you know, I really do make over 600k a year selling things to weak faggot commie fembois who are chronically online.
>>61988132

61988158
>>61988144
your fixation on 4chud memes and the "i've been found out, better attach an image to my post" behavior indicate otherwise.

someone who likely makes considerably more money than you is commanding you to answer a question. you'd better listen to your superiors.

61988165
>>61988158
Aww, that's cute. Your larp is funny. Someone's a little insecure about their wealth, aren't they?

61988211
>>61988132
Its definitely rich.

61988237
>>61988073
>>61987000
>>61987033

It's really all fantasy. Excluding a handful of high profile billionaires and celebrities you'd hardly know who to target. The rich are well protected in gated communities with private security and rapid police response if you did try to target big homes. Of course if you did target consumption the rich are quite capable of living with the appearance of restraint. I'm worth over $30MM and you couldn't pick me out as such most days

61988246
>>61988237
>the rich are untouchable gods!!! they are beyond any and all physical harm!!!!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V2iGJF0v0GI
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6sCioKnpHdY

61988409
>>61988237
> and you couldn't pick me out as such most days
Cause you don’t look good.
Growing up in a nice area, as kids, we would joke about richer kids and how richer kids looked different.

61988437
>>61988237
The only time rich people don’t look rich is when they are old.
Young rich people always look rich.

61988995
>>61988237
Anon, even the way you talk exposes you.
Also, you think you are safe, but you have no clue. These days tech can get someone really far... Just look at how wars are fought now, and how easy is to dox anyone in the world.

61992388
>>61987033
That's where Luigi's come in. Realistically speaking if the billionaires started getting whacked it would be steps closer to a revolution.
The cattle class are docile as a whole, so the other direction would be fear and chaos followed by an authoritarian Jewish police state

61992415
>>61988091
You can only be super rich if Jews let you, it's their platform, industry, and ultimately their country from the top down.
The only thing the cattle class can achieve is finding the nich shady spot on the plantation and milk it.

Amish maxing is the final solution to the societal disease

61992434
>He doesn't know all revolutions are started by the rich

61992443
>>61987000
Haahah if Im rich ill move somewhere im poor
Checkmate

61992488
>>61987000
Nice trips checked.
But nothing ever happens Anon. Least of all to the rich.

61993880
>>61992388
Clearly a lot of people cheered for that guy, but he basically sacrificed his life for nothing.
It has to be a collective action by anonymous people. That would give legitimacy and would also make rich people, politicians and their puppets shit their fucking pants

61993886
>>61987000
The kind of revolutionaries I'd be afraid of are on the same side as me. It's fine.

61995110
>>61988246
look at how the rothschild kike didn't even care about being confronted. He probably had a 5 snipers, 10 secret service fags disguised as civils, and 5 demons protecting him at all times.

61995115
>>61992434
Also, this. Not a single revolution has been started by a poorfag. Its always a figure sponsored by rich factions, or a kike.

61995465
>>61987006
This number includes IRAs and 401ks and doesn't take into account that if the owners tried to get to their assets, they'd owe the govt almost half of them. I wonder what the number is if you take taxes into account

61995581
We voted in a billionaire as our president.

Grow up man.

61995597
>>61987000
Poorfags can't overthrow the rich. Revolutions are done by millionaires against billionaires as poors die on both sides of the barricade

61995664
>>61988409
And what about the people you didn’t know were rich. You didn’t counter his point.

61996668
>>61987000
I went all in on gold and silver thanks anon. I was worried for a moment.

item.title: PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon — 139 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon

Open thread • Replies: 139 • Updated: 2026-03-17 07:43 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61981675
>>61981670
How is trump's cult spinning this one?

61981683
we are a net oil exporter

61981696
>>61981675
high oil prices are good now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990792
>>61990339
It's bots and shills, none of these people actually have a trading account. Certain political interests are paying bots and jeets to shit up every board.

Don't listen to a fucking thing anyone says unless they're providing screenshots of their actions.

61990829
>>61990792
I unironically hope the 2-week special combat mission ends soon so we can get back to our usual shills, jeets and bots. they were a bit calmer

61990840
>>61990829
The two week special combat operation is looking to last years at this point. My best guess is that the leaders of China and India are looking at food riots and total economic meltdown in 6 months. This forces them into military action or making some sort of deal with Iran.

I think the deal is more likely where they will pay a toll to have their oil released in exchange for cash/armaments. And since these nations cannot simply shut off the oil this deal will be made, regardless of what the US says.

61990861
>>61981670
WHY TF OIL DUMPING?!?!?!
this was supposed to be free money and yet I keep losing...

61990884
>>61990861

We haven't even begun to feel actual pain this is just speculation.

61990890
>>61990840
My portfolio can't stand this commotion. 2026/2027 was supposed to be a bullmarket according to Gann's Supercycle astrology ;____;

61990975
>>61990890
The assassination of Khomeni is looking like a generational foreign policy blunder you just have to roll with it.

61991002
>>61990083
>>61990078
nothing ukraine has achieved has been possible without NATO and the USA's direct and massive support in arms, material and leadership, the only thing Ukraine ever supplied was bodies for the meat grinder. Its disgusting frankly.

Ukraine lost the russian war on day 1, they lost all their jets before they left the ground, most of their bases were trashed and the majority of their vehicles were taken out before they even started fighting. It was then that NATO and us army forces pretending to be nato took over.

61991018
>>61990792
i have trading accounts on shares, but i don't really discuss them much, as every tip i ever got from here, and actually followed through on, was a dud and i lost money on it kek. Most of the posters here are clueless retards, i just like to stay up to date on the geopolitical-economic fronts and make my decisions based off of that.

61991050
>>61991018
Well yeah if you are going to make a play on oil getting to $150 you have to realize that it's a super risky move and don't get butthurt if there's suddenly a deal made and you lose all your money.

61991074
>>61983193
Yeah uh private credit definitely isn't imploading

61991122
>>61991018
>stay up to date on geopolitics and make decisions based on that
How's that fairing?

61991150
>>61991002
While I'm not pro-Ukraine or pro-Russia, I can't help but feel your pic related is directionally correct, although the Russian government also pushes globohomo "we need more niggers in our cities because muh diversity" bullshit in its own way.

61991170
>>61991002
Muscovy is already Snowmalia

61991172
>>61983218
Wow! What an active imagination you have. And what'll happen next, Junior?

61991455
>>61991122
its okay, i invest in mines, gold and JEPQ, that kike hedge fund that gives monthly returns on the NASDAQ. 8.7% atm, not bad. My mining companies in australia have gone great, but im no day trader, i buy and sit, i might sell shares every 6 months or so.

I got google at like $260 and sold at $310 last year. Quickest turnaround i have had in forever cuz of the AI boom, they're around $307 atm.

61991467
>>61991150
i completely agree with you, Russia is no opponent to globohomo, its just an opponent to this variation OF globohomo, as its dead-set on either beggaring their nation with the bullshit debt-currency, or destroying it when Putin kicked out the Central banking carteI kikes and the Ukraine war kicked off, im just in love with the speech itself frankly, what a way with words that general has/had.

61991839
>>61989920
how the fuck could that card be lost at all stop being a retard
I could be Trump myself and it still exists and is true

61991903
>>61991839
What's true? You're saying they are wrong because they are jewish. By that same logic I can say you're wrong cause you expect the orange zog puppet to lower prices.

61991952
>>61989859
The Strategic Reserve will be dry in a few weeks, what happens then domestically?

61992200
>>61991952
As I have said there's real pressure to resolve this issue and it will get exponentially worse the closer it gets to that point. Worst case scenario Iran keeps the pool closed and military action or war reparations will be completely necessary to prevent food shortages for billions of people.

61992285
>>61990078
and yet BLACK SEA'S CLOSED.
Which is what matters for us triple digits IQ oilchads monitoring the Hormuz strait situation.

61992307
>>61983193
Ain't even mad at the guy anymore; this is just funny.

61992316
>>61992285
What do you think the best play is? I have like 1 call on GUSH and 3 calls for WEAT exp oct but im looking to move another 2k into bets on this since I do not believe this will be resolved quickly

61992404
>>61992200
Will my oil positions go up more its -3% today or something like that :(

61994495
>>61991952
The US is currently getting everyone ready for boots on the ground.

61994533
>>61992404
>>61991952
There are multiple strategic reserves in the world for a total of billions of barils. It's a year of Hormuz traffic.
Whenever price go up they slowly put some in the market so they can milk the crisis without pushing the price down too much.
Expect some crabbing then a dump to 60 whenever they free the strait, might be today might take months.

61994542
When did they first find oil? In like 1909? And for centuries and millennia nobody needed oil. Nobody needs oil. We will be just fine.

61994545
>>61983193

61994692
>>61991952
drill more to fill in the blank you retard

item.title: Link TA — 13 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Link TA

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-17 22:25 UTC

OP

As we can see here chainlink just briefly eclipsed it’s all time high last cycle in January ‘25 and looks primed to smash through it this year. Fibonacci levels suggest targets between 25x and 60x from current ratios.
Do you think there will be any major news during the coming bullrun for chainlink or will all of the last five years worth of rocket fuel news just quietly start to ignite, forcing a market wide capitulation from air tokens and memes into the operating system of the industry?

Early replies (first 3)

61994708
FARRRRTtttt im ruining ur link thread XD fuck em all fuck em all hate stinkies WHY DO YOU HATE EM let me list the ways: they're so gay. they're so stinky. FUCK EM ALL ahahhahahhaha pathetic stinkbags YOU WILL NEVER KNOW HAPPINESS, FULFILLMENT, OR SATISFACTION. NONE OF THIS SHITTTT LAHAHAHahhahaaehehheheaahaah

61995068
>>61994673
I'm certain that a massive chain reaction of events will occur shortly after the clarity act is passed.
>the last five years worth of rocket fuel news just quietly start to ignite, forcing a market wide capitulation from air tokens and memes into the operating system of the industry
I just want to see how brad & charles react when this happens.

61995125
>>61994673
Hopium. Link will top out at $80 or something shit.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61995132
>>61995068
>brad
Rent free

61995219
>>61995125
That would be so cool!

61995454
>>61994673
Baggies are ridiculous

61996020
>>61994673
Thanks for the copium

61996065
>>61995068
Posts like this convince me absolutely NOTHING will happen.

Dumping my bags at 24, if I'm lucky.

61996475
Has anyone seen Sergey lately? Seems like they decided to pull him off the interview circuit for a while, maybe he’s busy telling the government what’s hwat

61997041
>>61996475
he and his pet chihuahua are in dc right now

61997046
>>61997041
hey now don't be rude, we are working very hard for you to make it you know bitch

61997154
>>61994673
>all time high surpassed november 2021
Price status?

61997253
>>61997046
A talking chihuahua!

item.title: BMW WON — 14 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BMW WON

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-17 22:13 UTC

OP

*is the only car company that didn't have a shitty 2025 year*

How did they do it /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

61996166
>>61996160
I still drive my 2014 BMW and I love it. It had hardly had any problems, but needs shocks and a valve cover replacement now.

61996174
>>61996166
I'm still driving the e46 m3 I retardedly bought when I was 18 lmao

61996178
>>61996160
BMW is not "E-car only" like the BBcock obsessed alternatives.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996649
I love my 120m. I know there are a lot of haters of the 1er facelift, but I love it.

61997050
>>61996174
What's it like being from a rich family?
And don't deny it, you bought an $80k car at age 18???

61997055
>>61996160
I drove a rented BMW on a trip recently and actually enjoyed it quite a lot. It had sick ass lane assist and radar cruise control that was much better than my wife's Toyota.

61997063
sold my bmw 3 years ago. I unironically prefer using the bus over it. people dont realize how annoying and time consuming a car can be especially as a person who doesnt give a fuck about cars and dont get me started on the monthly costs.

61997065
>>61996160
My gf bought a new ix3 and makes me drive. Ngl it’s nice and makes me consider buying one. They are hugely popular in Thailand right now.

61997110
>>61996649
i didnt believe that the grill could get any more ugly but somehow they did it. congratulations on this, seriously.

61997118
>>61997110
its much uglier in person since it feels and looks like really cheap plastic (which it is)

61997120
Yeah, only a 61% decline from 2022.

61997127
I've heard their EVs have shite range

61997175
>>61997050
An e46 m3 was not $80k in 2016 lmao

61997186
>>61996160
>top 10% own inflation-proof assets and therefore are profiting from inflation in k-shaped economy
>sell cars to top 10%
>profit

item.title: There are no finance jobs for introverts. — 11 replies — 8.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are no finance jobs for introverts.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-17 22:35 UTC

OP

There are no finance jobs for introverts. WTF, I went into this for the numbers and spreadsheets, not to stutter my way through pretending to be nice to strangers

Early replies (first 3)

61996203
>>61996183
Go into the buy side

61996230
>>61996203
This, unless your on like a structured products team you're a salesman.

61996246
>>61996203
Buy side doesn’t have an entry level, and my family isn’t Jewish, so there’s no easy in

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996258
>>61996183
Yeah I’m an accountant and although it is a lot of spreadsheets and excel and accounting systems and shit like that, there is also a fair amount of meetings and presentations and shit like that with sociopathic executive types, who can be very nice but are just so incredibly confident and outgoing that I feel like a cretin by comparison. I suppose I am though.

61996260
>>61996246
There is absolutely entry level, it's just with smaller firms. Hit indeed and start looking for RIA's

61996276
>>61996183
>There are no finance jobs for introverts.

There is but you have to be very good at it to have a chance. Few open slots and many "top graduate" applying for 100k + a year junior positions.

If you're not finding anything it's because you're not good enough compared to other candidates.

61996311
>>61996183
You won't get rid of meetings and socials in any area ever. Even the most autistic scientific institutions are ruled by politics and relationships. So you better up your social game because you're going nowhere without it.
>t. oldfag

61996330
finance is a total frat bro career these days. what did you expect?

61996374
>>61996183
>There are no finance jobs for introverts
This is every white collar job actually.
White collar is female coded, which is extrovert coded.

61996715
>>61996311
Yes. This is true

61997284
>>61996183
Nigga, finance is the psychopath central what were you expecting
